text:
# Company Leave Policy

## 1. Casual Leaves (CL)
Employees are entitled to 12 casual leaves per calendar year. Casual leaves are meant for urgent and unforeseen personal work. A maximum of 3 casual leaves can be taken consecutively. Unused casual leaves do not carry forward to the next year.

## 2. Sick Leaves (SL)
Employees are granted 8 sick leaves per year. If an employee is sick for more than 2 consecutive days, a medical certificate must be provided. Unused sick leaves can be accumulated up to a maximum of 20 days.

## 3. Privilege Leaves (PL)
Employees earn 1.5 privilege leaves for every month worked, totaling 18 days per year. Privilege leaves must be applied for at least 7 days in advance.

## 4. Maternity and Paternity Leaves
Female employees are eligible for 26 weeks of paid maternity leave. Male employees are entitled to 2 weeks of paid paternity leave, which must be availed within 6 months of the child's birth.

# Company Payroll Policy

## 1. Salary Payment Cycle
Salaries are disbursed on the last working day of every month. If the last day of the month falls on a weekend or public holiday, salaries will be credited on the preceding working day.

## 2. Salary Deductions
Standard deductions include Income Tax (TDS), Provident Fund (PF) which is 12% of basic salary, and Professional Tax as applicable per state laws. Any unapproved leaves (Leave Without Pay) will be deducted proportionally from the monthly salary.

## 3. Reimbursements
Employees can claim reimbursements for internet expenses (up to $50/month) and business travel. All claims must be submitted by the 20th of the month with valid receipts to be processed in the same month's payroll.

## 4. Bonus and Variable Pay
Annual performance bonuses are paid out in the month of April, based on the performance review cycle from January to December of the previous year. Variable pay is calculated quarterly and paid in the subsequent month.
